pg3-p1:
reference service. Our friends thought a lunacy commission should be appointed. Perhaps they were right. I had had some
success at speculation, so we had a little money, but we once worked on a farm for a month to avoid drawing on our small
capital. That was the last honest manual labor on my part for many a day. We covered the whole eastern United States in
a year. At the end of it, my reports to Wall Street procured me a position there and the use of a large expense account. The exercise of an option brought in more money, leaving us with a profit of several thousand dollars for that year.
pg3-p2-s1: For the next few years fortune threw money and applause my way.
pg3-p2-s3:
My judgment and ideas were followed by
many to the tune of paper millions.
pg3-p2-s4: The great boom of the late twenties was seething and swelling.
pg3-p2-s5:
Drink was taking an
important and exhilarating part in my life.
pg3-p2-s6: There was loud talk in the jazz places uptown.
pg3-p2-s7:
Everyone spent in thousands
and chattered in millions.
pg3-p2-s8: Scoffers could scoff and be damned.
pg3-p2-s9: I made a host of fair-weather friends.
pg3-p2:
For the next few years fortune threw money and applause my way. I had arrived. My judgment and ideas were followed by
many to the tune of paper millions. The great boom of the late twenties was seething and swelling. Drink was taking an
important and exhilarating part in my life. There was loud talk in the jazz places uptown. Everyone spent in thousands
and chattered in millions. Scoffers could scoff and be damned. I made a host of fair-weather friends.
pg3-p3-s1: My drinking assumed more serious proportions, continuing all day and almost every night.
pg3-p3-s2:
The remonstrances of my friends
terminated in a row and I became a lone wolf.
pg3-p3-s3: There were many unhappy scenes in our sumptuous apartment.
pg3-p3-s4:
There had been
no real infidelity, for loyalty to my wife, helped at times by extreme drunkenness, kept me out of those scrapes.
pg3-p3:
My drinking assumed more serious proportions, continuing all day and almost every night. The remonstrances of my friends
terminated in a row and I became a lone wolf. There were many unhappy scenes in our sumptuous apartment. There had been
no real infidelity, for loyalty to my wife, helped at times by extreme drunkenness, kept me out of those scrapes.
pg3-p4-s2:
We went at once to the country, my wife to applaud while I started out to overtake
Walter Hagen.
pg3-p4-s3: Liquor caught up with me much faster than I came up behind Walter.
pg3-p4-s4: I began to be jittery in the morning.
pg3-p4:
In 1929 I contracted golf fever. We went at once to the country, my wife to applaud while I started out to overtake
Walter Hagen. Liquor caught up with me much faster than I came up behind Walter. I began to be jittery in the morning. Golf permitted drinking
